immediate_actions: Call bank helpline immediately to block further transactions
Block the linked debit/credit card or UPI ID via your banking app
Report the unauthorized transaction to your bank within 24 hours to limit liability
evidence_required: Transaction ID and exact timestamp
Screenshot of bank SMS or notification
Phone number or UPI ID of the scammer (if known)
reporting_steps: File a formal complaint on the NCRP portal (cybercrime.gov.in)
Contact your nearest cyber police station with printed evidence
Submit a dispute form to your bank branch
prevention_tips: Never share OTPs, PINs, or passwords with anyone
Enable real-time transaction alerts for all accounts
Do not scan QR codes to receive money

immediate_actions: Check your registered email for any 'New Login' or 'Password Changed' alerts
Use the 'Forgot Password' or account recovery feature on the Instagram app
Immediately secure your linked email account by changing its password
evidence_required: Screenshot of suspicious login location/device
Link to your original profile URL
Records of the original registered email ID and phone number
reporting_steps: Report the hack via the Instagram Help Center (help.instagram.com)
Ask friends to report the account as 'Hacked' from their profiles
File a complaint on the national cyber crime portal if financial loss or defamation occurred
prevention_tips: Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) using an authenticator app
Use a strong, unique password for every social media account
Do not click on suspicious 'verified badge' or 'copyright infringement' links sent via DM

immediate_actions: Do not reply or engage with the bully/attacker
Block and mute the abusive accounts immediately
Document everything before reporting in case the attacker deletes the evidence
evidence_required: Screenshots of the abusive messages or comments (do not alter them)
Direct links (URLs) to the abusive posts
Profile URLs and usernames of the attackers
reporting_steps: Report the individual tweets and the abusive accounts directly to the platform
File a complaint on the national cyber crime portal under 'Cyber Bullying/Harassment'
Contact a legal professional if the abuse contains direct physical threats
prevention_tips: Consider making your profile private temporarily
Use platform features to restrict who can reply to your posts
Filter specific words or phrases in your account settings

immediate_actions: Call your bank's customer service to freeze the credit card instantly
Review your recent statements for any other unauthorized transactions
Change your net banking passwords immediately
evidence_required: Credit card statement highlighting the fraudulent charge
SMS or email alerts of unauthorized transactions
Details of the merchant name where the fraud occurred (if visible)
reporting_steps: Dispute the fraudulent charge with your bank immediately (often a zero-liability policy applies if reported promptly)
File a police report if required by the bank to process the dispute
Report to the cyber crime cell for investigation
prevention_tips: Do not save card details on untrusted or unknown websites
Disable international transaction capabilities when not traveling
Set up transaction limits for online and physical purchases

immediate_actions: Disconnect the infected device from the internet (Wi-Fi and Ethernet) immediately to prevent spreading
Do NOT pay the ransom, as there is no guarantee you will get your data back
Boot the system into Safe Mode to run diagnostic scans
evidence_required: Screenshot or picture (taken with another device) of the ransom note
Samples of the encrypted files (do not delete them yet)
The suspicious email, attachment, or link that likely caused the infection
reporting_steps: Report the incident to your national cyber emergency response team (e.g., CERT-In, CISA)
Seek professional incident response or IT support help
Check NoMoreRansom.org to see if a decryption key is publicly available for your specific ransomware
prevention_tips: Maintain regular, offline backups of your critical data (e.g., disconnected external hard drives)
Keep your operating system and antivirus software up to date
Never open suspicious email attachments or click unverified links

immediate_actions: Do not click on any links or download any attachments in the suspicious email
Do not reply to the sender or provide any personal information
If you already clicked a link and entered credentials, immediately change your password for that service
evidence_required: The original phishing email (do not delete it)
If possible, extract the email headers to identify the true sender
Screenshots of the fake login page (if you clicked the link)
reporting_steps: Mark the email as 'Phishing' or 'Spam' using your email provider's built-in reporting tool
Forward the phishing attempt to the anti-phishing working group or the impersonated organization's fraud department
Report to the national cyber crime portal if you lost data or money
prevention_tips: Always verify the sender's actual email address, not just the display name
Hover over links to see the actual destination URL before clicking
Enable 2FA on all important accounts

immediate_actions: Stop all communications and do not transfer any more money to the scammers
Do not pay 'withdrawal fees' or 'taxes' that they demand to release your supposed profits
Secure any legitimate cryptocurrency wallets by transferring funds to a new, secure wallet if possible
evidence_required: Screenshots of chat histories (Telegram, WhatsApp) where the investment was pitched
Transaction IDs (TxID) on the blockchain for any cryptocurrency sent
URLs of the fraudulent trading websites or apps
reporting_steps: File a complaint with the national cyber crime reporting portal with all crypto transaction details
Report the fraudulent exchange or wallet addresses to blockchain analysis firms or the platform where the scam originated from
Contact your local law enforcement agency specializing in financial crimes
prevention_tips: Be extremely wary of 'guaranteed' high returns with zero risk
Do not trust investment advice from random individuals contacting you on social media or messaging apps
Verify the credentials and regulatory licenses of any trading platform before investing

immediate_actions: Post on your real account to warn friends/family not to accept friend requests or send money to the fake account
Do not confront the fake account directly
Ask friends to mass-report the fake profile for impersonation
evidence_required: Link (URL) to the fake profile
Screenshots of the fake profile's page showing your photos and details
Screenshots of any malicious messages sent by the fake account to your contacts
reporting_steps: Use the platform's specific reporting tool for impersonation (e.g., 'Report -> Report Account -> It's pretending to be someone I know')
If the account is scamming people for money or posting defamatory content, report it to the cyber crime police
If it's a serious threat or involves identity theft offline, contact local authorities
prevention_tips: Regularly review your privacy settings and restrict profile visibility to 'Friends Only'
Watermark your personal photos if you are a public figure or are concerned about theft
Regularly search for your own name/images to catch fake profiles early
